findings.title: SECURITY DEFINER functions: search_path correctly pinned
findings.why_it_matters: Both SECURITY DEFINER functions use SET search_path = public, pg_temp. This is correct and prevents search_path hijacking attacks. No finding — flagging as verified-correct advisory.
evidence: Line 488: `SET search_path = public, pg_temp` on upsert_vocabulary_mastery
Line 573: `SET search_path = public, pg_temp` on search_similar_notes

findings.title: RLS FOR ALL policy permits DELETE on audio_bookmarks (immutable events)
findings.why_it_matters: audio_bookmarks is designed as an immutable event log (no updated_at, no mutation path). However, the RLS policy is FOR ALL (covers SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). A client with a bug or a compromised client could DELETE audio bookmark records. For true immutability, the policy should be split into INSERT + SELECT only — matching the flashcard_reviews pattern used for the other append-only table.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `FOR ALL` on audio_bookmarks with split INSERT WITH CHECK and SELECT USING policies (matching flashcard_reviews pattern at lines 184-194). Remove any UPDATE/DELETE policies.
evidence: Line 341: `CREATE POLICY "Users access own audio_bookmarks" ON public.audio_bookmarks FOR ALL ...`
Line 320: comment says `-- Audiobook chapter bookmarks. Immutable event — no updated_at column`
Compare lines 184-194: flashcard_reviews uses split INSERT+SELECT policies for its append-only design

findings.title: p_mastery_level has no bounds check in upsert_vocabulary_mastery
findings.why_it_matters: The vocabulary_items table has CHECK (mastery_level BETWEEN 0 AND 3). If upsert_vocabulary_mastery is called with p_mastery_level=-1 or p_mastery_level=5, the INSERT will fail with a CHECK constraint violation (SQLSTATE 23514) but only after the authz guard passes. More critically, the GREATEST logic for the ON CONFLICT path does not enforce the CHECK — a caller passing mastery_level=99 could try to set it higher (though the CHECK would catch it). A function-level validation guard before the DML would give a clearer error and prevent unnecessary DML that aborts.
findings.suggested_fix: Add before the INSERT: `IF p_mastery_level NOT BETWEEN 0 AND 3 THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'invalid mastery_level: must be 0-3' USING ERRCODE = '22023'; END IF;`
evidence: Line 292: `mastery_level INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (mastery_level BETWEEN 0 AND 3)` — DB-level constraint exists
Line 501: `mastery_level = GREATEST(vocabulary_items.mastery_level, EXCLUDED.mastery_level)` — no upper-bound guard on EXCLUDED.mastery_level

residual_risks: SECURITY DEFINER functions owned by the migration-running role (usually postgres superuser) — if the role changes, function privilege escalation could occur. Standard Supabase practice but worth noting in deployment docs.
book_highlights.flashcard_id is nullable UUID with no FK — if a flashcard is deleted by another user or in a concurrent session, stale UUIDs are silently preserved
testing_gaps: No test for unauthenticated (anon role) attempt to call upsert_vocabulary_mastery — should return 42501
No test for cross-user call to search_similar_notes (p_user_id differs from auth.uid()) — should return 42501
